page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 8 physical cores and 8 vCPUs, providing a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. The memory configuration consists of 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high resource density of 8.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that the ecs.se1ne.2xlarge delivers weak to average performance across various workloads. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as measured by stress-ng and Geekbench, fall into the bottom 25% tier. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for large-block uncached reads at 39.24 GB/sec and writes at 34.3 GB/sec, but small-block cached reads are weak. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, also register in the lower performance brackets, though the system maintains average capabilities in Passmark CPU scores and small-model LLM inference.

With a 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server is positioned as a memory-optimized option. The qualitative cost efficiency is characterized by its high memory capacity relative to compute power, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand high-frequency CPU performance. Due to the lack of local NVMe storage and weak database operation benchmarks, it is best suited for memory-cached applications, lightweight middleware, or memory-bound general-purpose tasks rather than high-throughput database hosting.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.2xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY physical cores (8 vCPUs) running at 2.5 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a KVM hypervisor and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks position this instance primarily in the weak-to-average range, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores landing in the bottom 25%, while large-block memory bandwidth and small-model LLM inference achieve average marks. Given its high 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.se1ne.2xlarge provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high-performance compute. It is best suited for memory-cached applications, lightweight middleware, and general-purpose workloads requiring a larger memory footprint.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1ne.2xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, designed for memory-intensive workloads without local storage.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for memory-cached applications and lightweight general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY physical cores at 2.5 GHz
64.0 GB of RAM providing a high 1:8 vCPU-to-memory ratio
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware
Best suited for memory-cached applications and lightweight middleware
